rows.name: vendor request
rows.html:
Greetings-

I am inquiring about becoming a vendor at the {{booking}}. I am a 3D Printer based in Dallas Oregon and specialize in printing of 3D dragons, frogs, turtles, and butterflies and much much more. I believe my unique creatures will be an asset to your event in 2024.

Please let me know what your process is for applying to be a vendor as well as any booth fees.

Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you soon.
rows.text:
Greetings-
I am inquiring about becoming a vendor at the {{booking}}.  I am a 3D Printer based in Dallas Oregon and specialize in printing of 3D dragons, frogs, turtles, and butterflies and much much more.  I believe my unique creatures will be an asset to your event in 2024.
Please let me know what your process is for applying to be a vendor as well as any booth fees.
Thank you for your time.  I look forward to hearing from you soon.

rows.event_name: Gleneden Beach Community Club Holiday Market
Handmade Crafts and Craft Supplies Sale

rows.dates_text: April 2024 (dates not updated) Event promoter has not updated for this year, last year's event was April 1 - 29, 2023

rows.note: emailed asking if the event offered electricity
Called and spoke with Melissa.  She gave me the name of the director Linda Griepentrog who will call or email me regarding the bazaar this year
App sent and accepted app on 1/26
Called and left message of Stacy.  I am wondering about load in times for the event.  I will email her as well asking.

Stacy called back and told us we can set up on Friday and they would have security all night, however, everyone has to be fully set up and ready to go by 10:00am on Saturday.  9-10 is the pet parade, then 10-4 is the bazaar / shows.
Called Sally today and left vm.  Asking about vendor information and holiday bazaar as well.
Talked to Sally, they have room for about 20 vendors.  Vendor fee is aprox 20 bucks.  6 foot tables spaces.  She will put me on the list for when she opens the list for the spring show
Emailed Linda.  She replyed saying she was getting the applications together and getting them ready to send out.  she asked if we wanted the same booth as last year and I said yes.
Got email with application from Sally, returned completed application.

Linda responded, Applications will be available in August.
Hi Jeremy,
Melissa sent me your contact info as an interested vendor for our Holiday Market. I'm in charge of it.

This year it's happening on November 18th. Information and booth applications will be available in August, so I'll add you to the mailing list.

In the interim, if you have any questions, let me know.

Thanks,


Linda Griepentrog
G Wiz Creative Services
PO Box 84 (for U.S. Mail)
7065 Neptune Ave. (street address)
Gleneden Beach, OR 97388
541-647-1529 phone
Sewnwrite@aol.com
Received application in the mail.  Will fill out and send back on Monday 5/8.  will also ask if I can pay via cc or cash

Hey Kim! This is the Dragon Guy that came in your shop a couple weeks ago. Hope you're doing well. I wanted to reach out because I'm really interested in doing consignment with your shop, Main Street Emporium. I've heard great things about your store and I think my products would be a great fit. I wanted to ask when you would be available to start the consignment arrangement. I'm excited to get started and work together to bring some unique items to your customers. Additionally, I wanted to inquire about the quantity of large dragons ($30.00) and medium dragons ($15.00) you would like to start with. Please let me know how many of each you would be interested in, so I can make sure to have them ready for you. Lastly, I wanted to mention that I will be participating in Krazy Days in July. It's going to be a fantastic event, and I think it would be a great opportunity for us to showcase our collaboration. Let's discuss further details and promotional strategies closer to the date. Looking forward to hearing from you and exploring this exciting consignment partnership. Thank you! Best regards, Jeremy Guill “The Dragon Guy” Dudefish Printing 503-990-4832 jeremy@dudefishprinting.com
Got a call from Carolyn today.  She had yet to received the application.  She said I could still get it in.  I will drop it off today
I dropped it off to Carolyns house today along with a 20 bill.  She gave me a receipt for the table feee
Emailed Sally asking for dates for Holiday bazaar this year
emailed them asking for an application and giving some pictures.
received an email in return.  They don't have info yet on the holiday market, they have added me to their list for when they do have info available.
received email yesterday with application.  I filled out and returned as of the writing of this note.
Contacted via their website to get more information.   Currently the webpage for the event is down on their website.

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to inquire about the Harvest Holiday Bazaar that is listed on your website for October 28, 2023, in the Jackman Long Building. However, it seems that your website for the event is currently experiencing technical difficulties.

I am interested in participating as a vendor at the bazaar and would greatly appreciate any information you can provide regarding the application process and requirements.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Best regards,

Jeremy Guill
Dudefish Printing
SENT COMPLETED APPLICATION TO INFO@MVCEA.COM TODAY

Dear Valued Vendor,

We cannot wait to see everyone this weekend!

Thank you again for your participation in the Homer Davenport Community Festival. 

Booth numbers and the festival map are attached. We did our best to accommodate specific requests. There will also be copies of the booth assignments at check-in at the information booth. 

For set up, all spaces will be marked and ready by 11:00am on Thursday August 3rd. There will be overnight security on Thursday night, Friday night, and Saturday Night. Vendors must be set up and ready for guests when the festival opens at 11:00am on Friday August 4th. 

Guests who did not receive a specific number will have first-come-first-serve booth assignments at check-in. 

The festival’s address is 300 Coolidge Street, Silverton OR 97381.

The Arts and Crafts Fair will be open from:

11:00am to 8:00pm on both Friday and Saturday August 4th and 5th. 

11:00am to 6:00pm on Sunday August 6th. 

We kindly ask that booths remain open during all hours of the Art and Craft Fair hours mentioned above.

Load out will begin after 6:00pm on Sunday, August 6th (after the festival closes). 

Please note that only a limited number of vehicles will be allowed in the loading lanes at any given time for loading and unloading purposes. You cannot leave your vehicle in the park and/or at your booth. We kindly ask that you work together and be mindful of others in the park.

Upon unloading, we request that you use the parking lot at the end of Hicks street by the pool. There is a walking path and bridge that will take you back to the park from the parking lot. Please note that the signs indicating permit parking only do not apply to this weekend.

If you requested electricity, please bring an extension cord that is at least 100 feet long. If you require an extension cord, please let us know before the event.

If you are a food vendor, please send a copy of your insurance with Homer Davenport Community Festivals, Silverton Rotary Foundation, and The City of Silverton as additionally insured to Vendors@homerdavenport.com.

Thank you again for your participation, and we look forward to seeing you at the festival.

Sincerely,

The Homer Davenport Community Festival Team

PS My name is Lari, and my cell phone is 303-493-1885 (call or text). There will also be committee members and volunteers in and around the park and at the Info booth during the festival as well.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Have a great Festival everyone!

Any other questions before then, please email Vendors@homerdavenport.com
Got email from Linda today, they are still looking for vendors.  I sent in my application to her this afternoon and alse sent her a check via bill pay (umpqua).

Dear Hop & Heritage Vendor,
 
Thank you for your recent application to our 2023 Hop & Heritage Festival.  This e-mail confirms our receipt of your application and the registration fees. This email indicates your application has been approved.
 
The Festival is a one-day event held on Saturday September 16th at Riverview Park and C Street in downtown Independence, Oregon. The event will be open to the public from 12-8 pm.
 
Booth space assignment(s) will be determined by seniority, your listed preferences, and other considerations made by Hop & Heritage vendor committee.   Your assigned booth space(s) will be marked in the park and on the street when you arrive the morning of September 16.
 
Please meet at the info booth, located near the fountain, to check in. You MUST check in before setting up your booth.
 
We will hand out an information sheet and give you your assigned booth space number.  Hop & Heritage volunteers will be wearing buttons to identify themselves.  They will be happy to help on the day of the event.
 
**Food vendors may start setting up at 8AM
 
**All other vendors may start setting up at 9:30AM
 
We have set times for vendors in order to reduce congestion. Please be mindful of the set up times.
 
We have over 65 vendors!  We will all need to work together and be patient.  Please be aware that this festival is entirely run by volunteers. This festival has grown considerably this year, and we are so excited that you are joining us. We would appreciate it if folks can unload, move their vehicles to the parking lot by the river, and then set up their booths.
 
All vendors should be set up by 11:45AM
 
For further information and clarification please reach out via email.

Myra Russell, Volunteer Vendor Coordinator

Independence Downtown Association

Email: info@downtownindependence.com 
Website: www.downtownindependence.com
RESERVED HOTEL conf:25616185 Ocean Terrace Condominiums (541-996-3623). $238.68 total

Good afternoon!

Another quick email about the Hop & Heritage Festival for Vendors based on some questions we’ve received!

Your vendor space is an empty 10x10 square on the ground.  You will need to provide your own tables, chairs, tent, display items as needed.  Unless you requested power or water on your application, you will not have access to power or water at your vendor space.
When you arrive Saturday, please go to the information booth first to get your vendor space assignment.  We will have maps available that morning. The information booth will be located at the top of the park near C Street & the Veteran’s Memorial.
Food Vendors may start setting up at 8AM and must be done setting up by 11:45AM.  You can set up any time during that time frame, just be aware that with 60+ vendors all trying to set up, you may not want to wait until the last minute.
All other vendors can start setting up at 9:30AM and must be done setting up by 11:45AM.  You can set up at any time during that time frame, just plan on being done by 11:45AM
All vendors will need to stay through the entire festival from 12-8PM
If you have further questions, please let me know!

Myra Russell, Volunteer Vendor Coordinator
Independence Downtown Association

Email: info@downtownindependence.com 
Website: www.downtownindependence.com
Apps should be ready around February
This is another note.  This note is substantially longer than the last note.  Lets see if thsi works for the purposes I am hoping they will work.  Fingers crossed!!
Sent an email asking for an application and apologizing for last bizarre's miscommunication.
Got email back.  Confirmed dates are correct.  Will send app as soon as it is available.
I received application from Sally this evening and returned it back to her filled out.
I emailed her and asked if I can pay via cc day of show
I emailed asking for an application open date and the fee schedule for 2023;
Called today, they dont have a director for 2024, they should select one by December.  Call back in January to get on the vendor list.
I checked again today, the ave the dates homepage was updated, but nothing on vendor info as of yet
Emailed asking for prices and timeframes for applications to be opened

Sent email to Kym asking about vendor information:  Greetings-

I am inquiring about becoming a vendor at the Polk County Fair 2024. I am a 3D Printer based in Dallas Oregon and specialize in printing of 3D dragons, frogs, turtles, and butterflies and much much more. I believe my unique creatures will be an asset to your event in 2024.

Please let me know what your process is for applying to be a vendor as well as any booth fees and deadlines.

Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Jeremy Guill
Dudefish Printing
503-990-4832
jeremy@dudefishprinting.com
Application should be open first week of February 2024
Emailed Sally asking about the Spring market

Emailed Hayley asking for information on Vendors.  She emailed back saying they are super close to putting out vendor applications and fees.  She will put me on the list to send out when they have it ready.
Emailed Tyler asking for dates and application openings
She is penciling us into booth 102

Greetings-

I am inquiring about becoming a vendor at the Newberg Fall Bazaar & Craft Fair. I am a 3D Printer based in Dallas Oregon and specialize in printing of 3D dragons, frogs, turtles, and butterflies and much much more. I believe my unique creatures will be an asset to your event in 2024.

Please let me know what your process is for applying to be a vendor as well as any booth fees.

Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Jeremy Guill
Dudefish Printing
503-990-4832
jeremy@dudefishprinting.com

LOADING AND UNLOADING:
There are 2 main loading areas and we also have access to a roll-up door. Since temps will most likely be a little cool in March we will try NOT to open the big roll-up doors unless absolutely necessary.  If you need the roll-up door opened, please find Troy or myself and we can help you.  
There are 4 doors that you can use to load and unload, 2 in the back, 1 on the right side of the building and the main entrance (see attached map).
All exterior doors (loading doors) will be monitored for safety and to prevent unauthorized access.

SCHEDULE:
Friday early vendor set-up is from 2pm – 6pm.  All loading doors will be unlocked during those hours.  Feel free to come and go as needed.  At 6pm we will start locking up.  

Saturday set-up will begin at 7:30am and continue until we open at 10am.  Please do not start tearing down your booth before 5pm.  
When you arrive, look for your folder with your name on it on your table.  It will include your vendor badges (they are actually wrist bands).  Please have everyone put on their wrist bands so we know who is who.

TEAR DOWN:
Please do not start packing up your booth before 5pm.  

The reason we are able to offer such affordable booth prices is because we have to do the cleaning and tear down all the tables ourselves.  So we are asking that each vendor pick up all the garbage in their area, on the tables, under the tables and put all garbage in the large garbage bins that are located throughout the building.  Vendors that do not clean up after themselves may not be invited back next year.

PARKING:
No charge for parking.  If you need to use the campground at the Expo center please call them directly to make arrangements and to pay for your camping site (503) 623-3048.

WHAT DO I NEED TO BRING:
The vendor tables are bare wooden tables, and you’ll need to bring your own table coverings.  Table size is 8 feet long by 2 feet wide.

Ice chests are welcome and subject to inspection.  No alcohol allowed on the premises, or we will get fined.  

If you don’t have a card reader or way to take card payments, there is an ATM on-site.

NEED MORE VENDOR BADGES (BANDS)?
You can buy as many as you want on the website (www.reptopiaexpo.com), and on the day of the expo they will no longer be on sale, and you’ll need to buy them at the Main Entrance.  So plan ahead if you think you might need to get more people inside at a discount.  Sorry no refunds will be given the day of the expo for unused vendor badges.
They had a last min opening... emailed me to get me signed up.
Was not selected as a vendor this year.
Got an email today saying our load in time is strictly at 8:30-9:00.  All vehicles need to be off premises by 9:00 am.

rows.name: sqlite_sequence
rows.sql: CREATE TABLE sqlite_sequence(name,seq)

rows.name: booking_receipt
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "booking_receipt"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    booking_fk   integer,
    filename     varchar(150),
    total        float,
    date_created datetime
)

rows.name: color_list
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "color_list"
(
    id   integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name varchar(100),
    hex  varchar(9)
)

rows.name: consignees
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "consignees"
(
    id         integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    company    varchar(100),
    name       varchar(100),
    title      varchar(50),
    address    varchar(150),
    address2   varchar(150),
    city       varchar(150),
    state      varchar(2),
    postalcode varchar(20),
    phone      varchar(20),
    email      varchar(150),
    active     tinyint
)

rows.name: consignment_products
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "consignment_products"
(
    id                 integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    consignment_sch_fk integer,
    product_fk         integer,
    price              float,
    qty                integer,
    qtySold            integer,
    discount           float,
    custom             tinyint,
    active             tinyint,
    date_added         datetime
)

rows.name: consignment_schedule
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "consignment_schedule"
(
    id                integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    consignee_fk      integer,
    effective_date    date,
    duration          integer,
    consignment_fee   float,
    cc_fee_percentage float,
    cc_fee_flat       float,
    contract          text,
    active            tinyint
)

rows.name: expenses
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "expenses"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    expense      varchar(75),
    amount       float,
    note         varchar(100),
    date_created datetime,
    date_updated datetime,
    bookingid    integer
)

rows.name: master_list
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "master_list"
(
    id            integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    event_name    varchar(150),
    january       tinyint,
    february      tinyint,
    march         tinyint,
    april         tinyint,
    may           tinyint,
    june          tinyint,
    july          tinyint,
    august        tinyint,
    september     tinyint,
    october       tinyint,
    november      tinyint,
    december      tinyint,
    first         tinyint,
    second        tinyint,
    third         tinyint,
    fourth        tinyint,
    last          tinyint,
    friday        tinyint,
    saturday      tinyint,
    sunday        tinyint,
    monday        tinyint,
    tuesday       tinyint,
    wednesday     tinyint,
    thursday      tinyint,
    updated       date,
    imagesrc      text,
    attendance    integer,
    exibitors     integer,
    website       text,
    dates_text    text,
    type_bazaar   tinyint,
    type_holiday  tinyint,
    type_art      tinyint,
    type_festival tinyint,
    type_flea     tinyint,
    type_health   tinyint,
    type_market   tinyint,
    active        tinyint,
    new           tinyint,
    date_created  datetime,
    date_updated  datetime,
    venuefk       integer,
    promoterfk    integer
)

rows.name: notes
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "notes"
(
    id            integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    note          text,
    date_created  datetime,
    date_updated  datetime,
    masterlistid  integer,
    bookingid     integer,
    consignmentid integer
)

rows.name: people
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "people"
(
    id         integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name       varchar,
    title      varchar(50),
    phone      varchar(20),
    fax        varchar(20),
    email      varchar,
    address    varchar,
    address2   varchar,
    city       varchar(75),
    state      varchar(2),
    zipcode    varchar(10),
    promoterfk integer,
    venuefk    integer
)

rows.name: printer
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "printer"
(
    id             integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name           varchar(100),
    manufacturer   varchar(100),
    model          varchar(100),
    purchase_date  date,
    purchase_price float,
    active         tinyint
)

rows.name: product
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "product"
(
    id                integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name              varchar(100),
    description       text,
    sku               varchar(100),
    sale_price        float,
    cost              float,
    threshold         integer,
    current_inventory integer,
    custom            tinyint,
    final_weight      float,
    final_print_time  float,
    picture           text,
    active            tinyint,
    created           datetime,
    updated           datetime
, items_per_plate integer default 1 not null)

rows.name: product_gcode
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "product_gcode"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    productfk    integer,
    printerfk    integer,
    filamentfk   integer,
    gcode        text,
    qtyInFile    integer,
    time_in_h    float,
    weight_in_kg float,
    cost         float
)

rows.name: product_misc
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "product_misc"
(
    id          integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    productfk   integer,
    packaging   float,
    advertising float,
    rent        float,
    overhead    float,
    extrafees   float,
    designhours integer
)

rows.name: product_warehouse
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "product_warehouse"
(
    id               integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    boxid            varchar(7),
    productfk        integer,
    date_filled      date,
    quantity         integer,
    heldforbookingid integer
)

rows.name: promoter
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "promoter"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name         varchar,
    address      varchar,
    city         varchar(100),
    state        varchar(50),
    zipcode      varchar(20),
    phone        varchar(20),
    website      varchar,
    date_created datetime,
    date_updated datetime
)

rows.name: report_side_menu
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "report_side_menu"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    catagory     integer,
    title        varchar(50),
    path         varchar(50),
    date_created datetime
)

rows.name: settings
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "settings"
(
    id                integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    cost_kW           float,
    default_markup    float,
    default_discount  float,
    padding_time      float,
    padding_filament  float,
    bizstarttime      text,
    bizendtime        text,
    mileagedict       blob,
    latestgasprice    float,
    latestgetgasprice date,
    mpg               float
)

rows.name: todo_bring
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "todo_bring"
(
    id        integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    item      varchar(150),
    duedate   date,
    todo      tinyint,
    complete  tinyint,
    bookingid integer
, section text)

rows.name: type
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "type"
(
    id            integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    type          varchar(50),
    densitygcm3   float,
    properties    text,
    useage        text,
    diameter      float,
    extruder_temp text,
    bed_temp      text,
    bed_adhesion  text,
    m_in_1kg_175  float,
    kW_hr         float,
    active        tinyint,
    m_in_1kg_3    float
)

rows.name: user
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "user"
(
    id              integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    firstname       varchar(150),
    lastname        varchar(150),
    company         varchar(150),
    title           varchar(50),
    about_me        text,
    address         varchar(150),
    city            varchar(150),
    state           varchar(2),
    postalcode      varchar(20),
    phone           varchar(20),
    email           varchar(150),
    dob             date,
    avatar_filename text,
    avatar_url      varchar(250),
    twitter         varchar,
    instagram       varchar,
    facebook        varchar,
    linkedin        varchar,
    username        varchar(150),
    password        varchar(150),
    date_created    datetime
)

rows.name: venue
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "venue"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name         varchar,
    address      varchar,
    city         varchar(100),
    state        varchar(50),
    zipcode      varchar(20),
    phone        varchar(20),
    website      varchar,
    latitude     float,
    longitude    float,
    date_created datetime,
    date_updated datetime
)

rows.name: filament_manufacturer
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE filament_manufacturer (
	id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
	company VARCHAR(100), 
	date_added DATETIME, 
	PRIMARY KEY (id)
)

rows.name: filament_manufacturer_spool_weight
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE filament_manufacturer_spool_weight (
	id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
	cardboard BOOLEAN, 
	plastic BOOLEAN, 
	spoolsize INTEGER, 
	weight FLOAT, 
	date_added DATETIME, 
	filmanfk INTEGER NOT NULL, 
	PRIMARY KEY (id), 
	FOREIGN KEY(filmanfk) REFERENCES filament_manufacturer (id) ON DELETE CASCADE
)

rows.name: filament
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "filament"
(
    id              integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    name            varchar(100),
    color1          varchar(100),
    colorhex1       varchar(20),
    color2          varchar(100),
    colorhex2       varchar(20),
    color3          varchar(100),
    colorhex3       varchar(20),
    priceperroll    float,
    aprox_remaining integer,
    spool_weight    integer,
    length_spool    integer,
    diameter        float,
    url             varchar(200),
    sku             varchar(100),
    purchasedate    date,
    picture         varchar(100),
    manufacturerfk  integer
        constraint filament_filament_manufacturer_id_fk
            references filament_manufacturer,
    typefk          integer,
    active          tinyint
)

rows.name: booking
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "booking"
(
    id                    integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    kd_appopen_new        date,
    kd_appopen_return     date,
    kd_appdeadline_new    date,
    kd_appdeadline_return date,
    cl_appsubmission      tinyint,
    cl_interested         tinyint,
    cl_appapproved        tinyint,
    cl_feepaid            tinyint,
    cl_electrequest       tinyint,
    cl_sendproductpic     tinyint,
    cl_sendlogo           tinyint,
    cl_sendbio            tinyint,
    info_wifipassword     varchar(100),
    info_datestart        date,
    info_dateend          date,
    info_timestart        time,
    info_timeend          time,
    info_loadindatetime   datetime,
    info_loadoutdatetime  datetime,
    info_boothfee         float,
    info_whatfeeincludes  text,
    info_boothlocation    varchar(100),
    cl_wifiavailable      tinyint,
    cl_foodavailable      tinyint,
    info_foodpurchase     varchar(100),
    days_remaining        integer,
    next_touch            date,
    active                tinyint,
    date_created          datetime,
    date_updated          datetime,
    eventid               integer,
    qty_taken             integer,
    qty_sold              integer,
    discount              float,
    qtySold               integer
)

rows.name: todoist
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE todoist (
	id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
	todoist_name VARCHAR(50), 
	"todoist_ProjectID" VARCHAR(50), 
	"todoist_SectionID" VARCHAR(50), 
	date_created DATETIME DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), 
	bookingid INTEGER, 
	PRIMARY KEY (id), 
	FOREIGN KEY(bookingid) REFERENCES booking (id)
)

rows.name: booking_products
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "booking_products"
(
    id         integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    booking_fk integer,
    product_fk integer,
    price      float,
    qty        integer,
    qtySold    integer  default 0,
    discount   float    default 0,
    custom     tinyint,
    active     tinyint,
    date_added datetime default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    shrinkage  integer  default 0 not null
, remaining integer default 0 not null, orders integer default 0 not null)

rows.name: booking_documents
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "booking_documents"
(
    id           integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    booking_fk   integer,
    filename     varchar(150),
    size         integer,
    type         varchar(5),
    date_created datetime default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)

rows.name: product_adjustments
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE "product_adjustments"
(
    id         integer
        primary key autoincrement,
    productfk  integer,
    adjustment integer,
    reason     varchar(100),
    date       date default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)

rows.name: item_filament_amount
rows.sql:
CREATE TABLE item_filament_amount (
	id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
	name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, 
	filament_amount FLOAT NOT NULL, 
	PRIMARY KEY (id)
)

rows.properties: Making durable parts that need to withstand higher temperatures, Easy to print with, strong plastic
rows.useage: Legos, instruments, sports equipment, Objects that might be dropped, knife handles, car phone mounts, phone cases, toys, wedding rings

rows.properties: Odorless, Low-warp, Eco-Friendly, Less energy to process
rows.useage: Food containers such as candy wrappers, biodegradable medical implants, models, proto type parts

rows.properties: Non harmful, non-toxic, and environment friendly, Easily be dissolved in water under normal temperature, Easily stripping.
rows.useage: paper adhesive, thickener, packaging film in feminine hygiene, adult incontinence products, childrens play putty or slime, freshwater sports fishing

rows.properties: Extremely durable and prints without odor. Has superior impact resistance that is superior to PET. Low shrinkage, no warping and not brittle.
rows.useage: Protective components like phone cases and mechanical parts that require flexibility. Food containers like cups and plates.

rows.properties: strong, lightweight, durable, flexible, wear-resistant, 100% thermoplastic
rows.useage: machine parts, mechanical components, structural parts, gears and bearings, dynamic load, containers, tools, consumer products and toys.

rows.properties: Versatility, Real wood scent, Durability, contain real wood fibers
rows.useage: wooden box, wooden figurine, tables, chairs, cups or the likes.

rows.properties: Highly Durable, Soluble, Low Warpage, Good layer adhesion
rows.useage: Frames, supports, propellers, tools, mechanical parts, protective casings, shells, high durability applications

rows.properties: Flexible 3D printing material, excellent abrasion resistance, smooth feeding properties, Durability
rows.useage: Toys, novelty items, wearable, phone cases, visual products.

rows.properties: Odorless, Low-warp, Eco-Friendly, Less energy to process
rows.useage: Food containers such as candy wrappers, biodegradable medical implants, models, proto type parts
